title: Mental Health | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## New community beds opening ahead of schedule in Waikato
Four new community based forensic step-down beds will open ahead of schedule in March thanks to the partnership between Ember Services Limited and Health NZ.

## Mental health co-response teams coming to first four regions
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey today announced the first four new mental health co-response team locations that will work alongside Police to respond to 111 calls.

## Tauranga emergency department gets peer support workers
The Government has delivered on its commitment to roll out peer support services in eight emergency departments five months ahead of schedule, with Tauranga Hospital becoming the latest hospital to implement the service.

## Vacancy rates fall as frontline mental health workforce grows
New workforce data shows the frontline mental health and addiction workforce has grown significantly and vacancy rates have decreased under this Government, Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey says.

## Community boost strengthens focus on prevention
Thousands of New Zealanders will have faster access to mental health support following a funding boost for four community organisations, Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey says.

## New specialist mental health and psychogeriatric beds open in Lower Hutt
The Government is delivering faster access to mental health support with the addition of 31 new specialist beds at Manor Park Private Hospital in Lower Hutt.

## Mental health targets delivering faster access to support
New data released today shows strong improvement across all three mental health and addiction access targets compared with the same quarter last year, Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey says.

## New Plymouth crisis recovery café gets a boost
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey today visited Koha Café where he announced New Plymouths local crisis recovery café, will receive a funding boost from the Government.

## Compassionate change underway for stillbirth families
The Government has begun work on making the process of registering a stillbirth more compassionate and supportive for grieving parents, Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey says.

## New wraparound counselling service to mark World AIDS Day
Mental Health and Associate Health Minister Matt Doocey today hosted a World AIDS Day event at Parliament, announcing new funding to help more New Zealanders have faster access to mental health support.

## Top Up exceeding expectations and reaching millions
The nationwide Top Up campaign, launched in August to encourage Kiwis to take everyday actions that boost mental wellbeing, has reached 3,283,076 people and achieved a milestone of 115,000 website visitors in its first four months.

## AI navigation to deliver faster access to support
New Zealanders seeking mental health support will soon have a better understanding of what services are available to them, thanks to government funding for a mental health AI navigation platform.

## New actions to grow the mental health and addiction workforce
The Government is delivering on reducing wait times and growing the frontline mental health and addiction workforce, one year on from the country’s first dedicated workforce plan.

## More people to get faster access to support
Hundreds of extra young people, mums, and families across New Zealand will now get faster access to mental health support, following a boost of funding for multiple community organisations.

## Gumboot Friday continuing to deliver results
Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey is pleased to celebrate Gumboot Friday today, a fantastic initiative helping thousands of young people access free mental health support faster.

## Delivering a better mental health crisis response
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey has today announced the government is delivering more frontline workers and establishing new services for people in need of a better crisis response.

## New Mental Health and Wellbeing Commission Board appointments
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey has announced two new appointments to the Mental Health and Wellbeing Commission Board, alongside a new Deputy Chair and two reappointments.

## Whakatāne crisis recovery café gets boost
Whakatāne marks the thirteenth and final stop on the Rural Health Roadshow. Minister Doocey attended a public meeting today to hear from the local community and the frontline.

## Peer support workers join North Shore Hospital
Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey has welcomed the introduction of peer support workers in North Shore Hospital’s Emergency Department, completing the rollout of the service across all three major Auckland hospitals.

## AI-powered mental health support tool launched
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey and Sir John Kirwan today launched Ask Groov, Health New Zealand’s first endorsed AI powered wellbeing guide for adults.

## Peer support workers join Dunedin Hospital’s frontline
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey has celebrated the addition of peer support workers in Dunedin Hospital’s Emergency Department today with a visit to the region to meet some of the team.

## Launch of new Top Up Community Grants
New Top Up Community Grants to boost local mental health initiatives have been launched as part of Mental Health Awareness Week 2025.

## New data shows faster access to mental health support delivered
With quarter four data now in, for the first time we can compare a full year of data to measure how well mental health and addiction services are performing across the country, Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey says.

## New Mental Health Unit opens in Palmerston North
Minister for Mental Health Matt Doocey today officially opened Ngā Wai Ngāro, a new acute mental health facility at Palmerston North Hospital.
